chunk_summary: A structured checklist for outpatient stunting visits, based on the UNICEF framework, addresses multidomain determinants like food security, caregiving, maternal health, WASH, and program access, enabling actionable clinic interventions and follow-up outcomes.
learning_objectives: Understand the multidomain determinants of stunting using the UNICEF framework.
Learn to implement a structured checklist for outpatient stunting visits in under two minutes.
Identify key barriers to child nutrition, including food security, caregiving constraints, and environmental risks.
Evaluate follow-up outcomes such as improved food security and reduced diarrhea episodes.
key_facts: The checklist can be completed in under two minutes during outpatient visits.
Food security is assessed using the two-question Hunger Vital Sign tool.
Maternal health screening includes anemia treatment, postpartum depression, and caregiver stress.
WASH factors include water source, toilet access, and handwashing practices.
Environmental risks like household air pollution and solid fuel use are documented.
Program access includes anganwadi nutrition, school meals, and deworming/IFA supplementation.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: UNICEF conceptual framework on maternal and child nutrition — https://www.unicef.org/media/113291/file/UNICEFConceptualFramework.pdf
document_key_topics: Multidomain assessment using the UNICEF framework
Structured checklist for outpatient stunting visits
